commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=94cdcafaa3e17cfcf7c142bf28e2488a9c51a947)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Google Gemini CLI auth alias, expands Gemini CLI onboarding runtime bindings across Gemini chat models, and adds regression tests for re-onboarding from another CLI provider.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +419. Total +437 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level, not as a live run here. Current main binds only the default Gemini CLI model and lacks the manifest auth alias needed for fallback auth-profile matching, while the linked issue reports the matching re-onboarding hang.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Live proof depends on a real Gemini CLI OAuth setup, and the catalog ownership concern needs maintainer or contributor follow-up before merge.

target.title: fix(google): add google-gemini-cli auth alias and expand model runtime bindings
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
closes #95851
After re-onboarding from another provider (e.g. Claude CLI) to Gemini CLI via `openclaw onboard`, the chat becomes unresponsive — the UI shows infinite loading and never returns a response. The onboarding completed successfully (auth tokens stored, config written), but sending a message hangs indefinitely.

## Why This Change Was Made

- Adds `"providerAuthAliases": { "google-gemini-cli": "google" }` to the Google extension manifest so `resolveProviderIdForAuth("google-gemini-cli")` returns `"google"`. Without this alias, the auth profile fallback path in `resolveCliRuntimeFromAuthProfile()` cannot match `google-gemini-cli` profiles to the `google` provider, returning `undefined` and falling through to a direct Google API call without valid credentials.
- Expands the Gemini CLI config patch from a single model (`google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`) to all 7 active Gemini chat models. When users pick a model not in the original patch, the `agentRuntime` binding is absent from the `models` map, forcing the broken auth profile fallback path.
- Keeps existing Gemini CLI onboarding for first-time users and direct Google API key auth unchanged.

## User Impact

Users switching from another provider to Gemini CLI via `openclaw onboard` now get working chat responses instead of infinite loading. New Gemini CLI setups and existing Claude CLI / Google API key configs are unaffected.

## Evidence

- Branch base checked during freshness gate: `origin/main` at `ad3b2f4b88`.
- Real behavior proof:
  - **Behavior or issue addressed:** `resolveCliRuntimeExecutionProvider` returns `"google-gemini-cli"` for all 7 Google Gemini models after Gemini CLI onboarding, and `resolveProviderIdForAuth("google-gemini-cli")` resolves to `"google"`.
  - **Real environment tested:** Linux Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw `main` branch, branch `fix/gemini-cli-auth-alias-reonboard`.
  - **Exact steps or command run:**
    ```bash
    pnpm test src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.reonboard-gemini-cli.test.ts --run
    ```
  - **Evidence after fix:**
    ```text
    Test Files  1 passed (1)
         Tests  17 passed (17)
    ```
    CI integration test covers:
    - Config patch shape: all 7 active Gemini chat models get `agentRuntime: { id: "google-gemini-cli" }`
    - `providerAuthAliases`: `google-gemini-cli` → `google`, canonical `google` unchanged
    - Post-onboarding config: old Claude CLI entries preserved, primary model updated, Gemini CLI auth profile stored
    - Runtime resolution: all 7 Gemini models resolve to `"google-gemini-cli"` via configured runtime path
    - Auth profile fallback: model without explicit `agentRuntime` still resolves via auth profile (thanks to providerAuthAliases)
    - Backward compat: old Claude CLI models still resolve to `"claude-cli"`
  - **What was not tested:** Live Gemini CLI OAuth onboarding with a real Google account sending a chat message end-to-end. The CI test exercises the production `resolveCliRuntimeExecutionProvider` and `resolveProviderIdForAuth` functions with real config shapes, but the full OAuth flow and Gemini CLI process execution are not exercised.
- Regression tests and local checks:
  ```text
  pnpm test extensions/google/manifest.test.ts extensions/google/index.test.ts src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.test.ts src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.reonboard-gemini-cli.test.ts src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --run
  Test Files  6 passed (6)
  Tests  97 passed (97)
  ```

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit + CI integration tests
- Target test files:
  - `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.reonboard-gemini-cli.test.ts` **(new)** — 17 tests simulating full re-onboarding scenario
  - `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.test.ts` — 1 new test: Gemini CLI runtime binding resolves correctly
  - `src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts` — 1 new test: `google-gemini-cli` → `google` alias resolution
  - `extensions/google/manifest.test.ts` — 1 new test: manifest declares `providerAuthAliases`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - `resolveCliRuntimeExecutionProvider` resolves `"google-gemini-cli"` for all 7 Gemini models after re-onboarding
  - `resolveProviderIdForAuth("google-gemini-cli")` returns `"google"` via the manifest `providerAuthAliases`
  - Google manifest declares `providerAuthAliases: { "google-gemini-cli": "google" }`
  - Old Claude CLI models preserved in merged config
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bugs are in (1) the manifest metadata declaration and (2) the config patch shape generated during onboarding. The new tests directly verify both points at the level where the bug lived, plus a full integration test simulating the merged config state.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Two interacting gaps. (1) The Google extension manifest had no `providerAuthAliases` entry mapping `google-gemini-cli` → `google`, so `resolveCliRuntimeFromAuthProfile()` could not recognize `google-gemini-cli` auth profiles as compatible with the `google` provider — the fallback returned `undefined` and the runtime attempted a direct Google API call without valid credentials. (2) The Gemini CLI config patch only set `agentRuntime` for `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, so any other Gemini model selected during model picking had no runtime binding and fell through to the broken auth profile fallback.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests covered Anthropic/Claude CLI runtime resolution but not the Gemini CLI `resolveCliRuntimeExecutionProvider → resolveCliRuntimeFromAuthProfile → resolveProviderIdForAuth` path, and no test verified the config patch covered all active models.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
